topic: Introduction to JavaScript classes, object instantiation using constructors and methods, and modularizing code by moving class definitions into separate files.

concepts.name: class
concepts.explanation: A class is described as a 'design plan' or a 'template' rather than the object itself. It provides the blueprint for what an object will look like and what it can do.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows you to create many unique objects that all follow the same rules, making your code organized and reusable.

concepts.name: constructor
concepts.explanation: A special function that runs automatically when a new object is created. It takes in data (like x, y coordinates) and assigns it to the specific object.
concepts.why_it_matters: It allows each individual instance of a class to have its own unique properties (like position or size) even though they come from the same template.

concepts.name: this keyword
concepts.explanation: The instructor explains 'this' as a way of saying 'the X that you give me this time is used only for this particular object.' It refers back to the specific instance being worked on.
concepts.why_it_matters: Without 'this', you would be changing global variables instead of the specific properties of an individual object.

concepts.name: methods
concepts.explanation: Functions defined inside a class that allow an object to perform actions, such as moving or displaying itself.
concepts.why_it_matters: Methods allow objects to have 'behaviors' that are tied directly to their own data.

concepts.name: file modularization
concepts.explanation: The process of moving a class definition out of the main sketch file and into its own dedicated .js file to keep code clean.
concepts.why_it_matters: It makes your code modular and allows you to easily share classes across different projects.

code_examples.demonstrates: A complete class definition including a constructor for properties and methods for behavior.
code_examples.common_issue: Forgetting to use 'this.' before property names inside the class, which would cause errors or incorrect behavior.

code_examples.demonstrates: How to link multiple JavaScript files in an HTML document so the main sketch can access external classes.
code_examples.common_issue: Linking files in the wrong order (the class file must be loaded before the sketch that uses it) or misspelling the filename.

mistakes_and_fixes.cause: The class was moved to a separate file, but the HTML file wasn't updated to include a <script> tag for that new file.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Add a `<script src="UFO.js"></script>` tag in the index.html file.

mistakes_and_fixes.symptom: The loop variable is being treated as an object property incorrectly.
mistakes_and_fixes.cause: Using `this.x` inside a loop that is iterating over an array of objects, rather than using the local iterator variable.
mistakes_and_fixes.fix: Use the loop variable directly when you aren't referring to a specific object property.

key_moments.description: The instructor uses the 'design plan' analogy to explain classes, which helps bridge the gap between abstract code and tangible objects.
The instructor intentionally breaks the code to demonstrate how a ReferenceError occurs when files aren't linked correctly in HTML.
Explaining the structure of HTML (head vs body) to show where scripts and styles should live.

prerequisites: Basic understanding of the p5.js Web Editor interface
leads_to: Complex generative systems using nodes and walkers
student_questions: How do I make many of the same thing without writing the code 80 times?
Why is my code saying 'is not defined' after I moved it to a new file?
What does the 'this' keyword actually do?
How do I use a class to make objects move?
What is the difference between a class and an object?
How do I connect my JS file to my HTML file?
Why are all my objects appearing in the same spot?
How do I make a template for a shape?
